page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.cm4.4xlarge is an x86_64 architecture server instance powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2667 v4 processor running at 3.2 GHz. It features 16 dedicated physical cores with 1 thread per core, providing a total of 16 vCPUs. The hardware configuration includes 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying entirely on network-attached storage and baseline network bandwidth of 6 Gbps. Virtualization is managed via the KVM hypervisor.

Benchmark data indicates average overall performance across most standard workloads. The multi-core CPU performance, measured via stress-ng at 11469.52, and the PassMark CPU score of 14861.27 both fall within the middle tier of tested servers. However, single-core performance is relatively weak, with a stress-ng single-core score of 1150.39. Memory read bandwidth is average, reaching 834.57 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 54.29 GB/sec for large blocks, though large block write bandwidth is weaker at 24.73 GB/sec. The instance handles database operations and web serving adequately, demonstrating a Redis SET throughput of 12.97 million operations per second and static web serving throughput of 10.85 GB/sec.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the E5-2667 v4 processor represents an older hardware generation, which may offer a lower entry cost for dedicated core resources but exhibits lower single-threaded efficiency compared to newer architectures. The absence of AVX-512 instructions limits its utility for advanced vector math or modern machine learning acceleration, as reflected in the weak prompt processing speed of 22.12 tokens per second for 7B LLM models. This instance is best suited for general-purpose workloads, multi-threaded applications, static web serving, and moderate database operations that benefit from dedicated vCPUs and a balanced memory-to-core ratio without requiring local SSD storage or high-performance single-core execution.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.cm4.4xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 16 dedicated physical cores on an Intel Xeon E5-2667 v4 processor running at 3.2 GHz. It is configured with 64.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 4.0 GB per core, and operates on the KVM hypervisor. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, relying on a baseline network bandwidth of 6 Gbps. Benchmarks show average multi-core CPU and memory read performance, though single-core execution and large-block memory write speeds are relatively weak. With a PassMark CPU score of 14861.27 and Redis throughput of 12.97 million operations per second, this instance is well-suited for multi-threaded general-purpose applications, static web serving, and moderate database workloads that do not require modern instruction sets like AVX-512 or high single-threaded performance.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.cm4.4xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 16 dedicated Intel Xeon cores, 64 GB RAM, and 6 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering average multi-core and database performance.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.cm4.4xlarge features 16 dedicated Intel Xeon cores and 64 GB RAM, offering balanced general-purpose multi-core performance.
tagline: A dedicated 16-core x86 server offering balanced memory-to-CPU allocation for standard multi-threaded workloads and general-purpose applications.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated physical Intel Xeon E5-2667 v4 cores at 3.2 GHz
64.0 GB of system memory providing a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
6 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with KVM virtualization
Average multi-core CPU, database, and static web serving benchmark performance
Absence of local storage and GPU acceleration
Suited for general-purpose multi-threaded applications and moderate database tasks
